_comment: B2 (Fable): the global kill-switch. Action Plan §6 promised counsel a red button; it did not exist - visual_personalization was per-record, so turning everything off meant 37 file edits under pressure. This file is the button. generate.js reads it at render; global:false suppresses the Higgsfield visual layer on EVERY surface regardless of per-record flags, falling back to the locked navy gradient hero. Per-surface entries can force a single surface off without touching the rest.

visual_personalization._surfaces_note: Add "surface-id": false to disable one surface. Absent = follow the record's own media.visual_personalization value. Present and false = forced off regardless of the record.
visual_personalization._last_verified: 2026-07-22 (fallback tested 4 scenarios: global-on, global-off, per-surface-off, file-missing)
visual_personalization._verified_by: Opus 4.8 remediation pass; pending Fable delta re-check
